content.content:
{"body":"Please provide as much information as you can: 

* **Suggested term label:**

 copper ion import into the Golgi

* **Definition (free text)**

The process in which copper is transported from the cytosol into the Golgi apparatus. 

* **Reference, in format PMID:#######**

https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/9325307/
Restriction of copper export in Saccharomyces cerevisiae to a late Golgi or post-Golgi compartment in the secretory pathway

https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/7708696/
The Menkes/Wilson disease gene homologue in yeast provides copper to a ceruloplasmin-like oxidase required for iron uptake
These results suggest that the CCC2-encoded protein is required for the export of copper from the cytosol into an extracytosolic compartment, supporting the proposal that intracellular copper transport is impaired in Wilson disease and Menkes disease.



* **Gene product name and ID to be annotated to this term**

https://www.pombase.org/gene/SPBC29A3.01

* **Parent term(s)**

GO:0035434  copper ion transmembrane transport
.

* **Any other information**


curating  https://www.pombase.org/reference/PMID:15107426
which stated:
Atx1 shuttles copper from the
cytosol to post-Golgi vesicles by specifically docking with the
Ccc2 copper-transporting P-type ATPase (32). Once loaded,
Ccc2 subsequently pumps copper into the lumen of the Golgi, to
metallate the copper-dependent ferroxidase Fet3 (32, 33). Al-
though ATX1 and CCC2 gene transcription is unaffected by
intracellular copper status, these genes are transcriptionally
activated in response to iron starvation, illustrating their im-
portance to iron metabolism through the delivery of copper to
Fet3 (23, 34)
","comments":[{"author":"ValWood","body":"@dragon-ai-agent  please create new GO BP term
copper ion import into the Golgi
Definition :The process in which copper is transported from the cytosol into the Golgi apparatus.
database xrefs PMID:9325307 PMID:7708696
parent: GO:0035434 copper ion transmembrane transport
","createdAt":"2026-04-01T08:50:27Z"},{"author":"dragon-ai-agent","body":"🤖 Working on it...

Follow along: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/23840275650)

*— @dragon-ai-agent*","createdAt":"2026-04-01T08:50:51Z"},{"author":"dragon-ai-agent","body":"New term created: **copper ion import into Golgi lumen** (`GO:7770057`)

**Definition:** "The directed import of copper cation from the cytosol across the Golgi membrane into the Golgi lumen."

The term follows the standard ion import into organelle design pattern (modeled on `GO:1904257` zinc ion import into Golgi lumen). The logical definition will allow the reasoner to automatically classify this under `GO:0035434` (copper ion transmembrane transport) and `GO:0015677` (copper ion import).

I adjusted the label slightly from your suggestion to "copper ion import into Golgi lumen" to match the established naming convention for this pattern (cf. "zinc ion import into Golgi lumen"). Your requested name "copper ion import into the Golgi" is included as an EXACT synonym.

Both PMIDs validated:
- **PMID:9325307** - Yuan et al. (1997) - Restriction of copper export in *S. cerevisiae* to a late Golgi or post-Golgi compartment
- **PMID:7708696** - Yuan et al. (1995) - The Menkes/Wilson disease gene homologue in yeast provides copper to a ceruloplasmin-like oxidase

PR: #31795
